content:
The user wants me to download an Instagram post. The URL is https://www.instagram.com/p/CxAbcExample/ — this appears to be a placeholder/example URL.

Looking at the instructions, I need to:
1. Start by reading the current page to understand the context
2. The user wants to download an Instagram post
3. According to the "SOCIAL MEDIA DOWNLOADS" section, when the user asks to download images or videos from Instagram, I should call `download_social_media` — it's a SINGLE tool call that handles the per-site DOM, picks the right resolution, and saves to the Downloads folder.

Let me first check what's on the current page, then use the download_social_media tool.

The current page context shows: URL: https://www.instagram.com/p/CxAbcExample/ — Title: Photo — Instagram

So I'm already on an Instagram post page. I should use the download_social_media tool directly since that's what the instructions say for Instagram downloads.[
    {"tool_call_id": "0", "tool_name": "download_social_media", "parameters": {"url": "https://www.instagram.com/p/CxAbcExample/"}}
]
